title: Why ADHD women can't get out of bed in the morning
overlayTitle: Alarm goes off. You still can't move?
description: If mornings feel impossible no matter how early you set your alarm, it might not be laziness - it could be ADHD. Discover what's really going on. Take the free ADHD quiz at bloomfocus.org/quiz
imagePrompt: Realistic aesthetic photograph, cozy bedroom flat-lay, soft natural morning light filtering through sheer curtains, muted pastel tones (lavender, cream, blush). A rumpled linen duvet, a phone face-up showing multiple snooze alarms, a half-drunk glass of water on a wooden nightstand, small potted succulent nearby. Shallow depth of field, film-like grain, warm golden hour glow. No people, no faces, no text. Vertical 2:3 portrait 1000x1500.

title: Signs your chaotic morning routine is actually ADHD
overlayTitle: Ready in 2 hours, still forgot everything?
description: Spending hours getting ready and still walking out frazzled is a classic ADHD pattern in women - and most don't know it. Is this ADHD or just stress? Take the free test at bloomfocus.org/quiz
imagePrompt: Realistic aesthetic photograph, cluttered but cozy entryway flat-lay, soft diffused natural light, muted pastel tones (sage, cream, dusty rose). A canvas tote bag spilling out its contents - lip balm, a crumpled to-do list, tangled earbuds, a planner with sticky notes - on a light wood floor. A single key fob lying apart from a keychain. Shallow depth of field, film-like texture. No people, no faces, no text. Vertical 2:3 portrait 1000x1500.

title: Can't start your morning tasks even with a routine plan
overlayTitle: Perfect routine set. Still can't start it?
description: You've tried every morning routine on Pinterest and still can't follow through. That's not a willpower problem - it could be ADHD. Find out in 2 minutes. Take the free ADHD quiz at bloomfocus.org/quiz
imagePrompt: Realistic aesthetic photograph, desk flat-lay in soft morning light, muted pastel tones (lavender, cream, warm white). An open bullet journal with a neatly written morning routine checklist - all boxes empty and unchecked - beside a ceramic mug of coffee with light steam, a capped pen resting across the page, and a small vase with dried pampas grass. Phone screen faintly visible showing a social media feed. Shallow depth of field, film-like grain. No people, no faces, no text. Vertical 2:3 portrait 1000x1500.

title: ADHD morning routine when you can't get started

headline: 5 ADHD Morning Wins That Stick
title: 5 ADHD Morning Routine Hacks That Actually Work
description: ADHD mornings don't have to be chaos. These 5 simple strategies reduce decision fatigue, boost dopamine, and help you start the day with momentum. Take the free ADHD quiz at bloomfocus.org/quiz
imagePrompt: Realistic aesthetic photograph, minimalist cozy desk corner with a soft linen placemat, a small pastel mug, and a single dried flower stem, lots of empty negative space at center and top, soft warm natural window light, muted blush and cream tones, no people, no text. Vertical 2:3.
chatgptPrompt:
Create a clean Pinterest infographic (vertical 2:3, 1000x1500) for an ADHD wellness brand "bloom focus".

TITLE (large, bold, friendly rounded font at top): "5 ADHD Morning Wins That Stick"

Numbered list with a small matching pastel icon next to each item:
1. Alarm + body doubling video
2. One-minute movement burst
3. Visual checklist on mirror
4. Same breakfast, zero decisions
5. Reward yourself for showing up

Style: soft pastel palette (lavender, cream, sage green, blush pink, light blue), cute flat illustrations, rounded legible sans-serif, soft decorative elements (stars, leaves, hearts), warm supportive feeling. Add "bloomfocus.org" small at the bottom center. Make sure all text is spelled perfectly and the numbers run 1 to 5 in order.

headline: ADHD Morning Brain Freeze
items: Know what to do, still frozen
title: ADHD Executive Dysfunction Morning Signs
description: ADHD Executive Dysfunction makes mornings brutally hard - not laziness, just a brain that struggles to initiate. Recognize these signs in yourself? Take the free ADHD quiz at bloomfocus.org/quiz

title: ADHD task initiation kit to stop morning paralysis

memeText:
My alarm: 7am
My brain: okay but what if we just think about everything first
title: Relatable ADHD morning memes that hit too hard
description: ADHD mornings hit different when your brain wants to overthink before you've even sat up. Time blindness is real, friend. 😅 More at bloomfocus.org
imagePrompt: Realistic aesthetic photograph, soft pastel tones (lavender, cream, blush, sage), cozy minimal scene, lots of empty space, soft natural light, shallow depth of field. No people, no text. Vertical 2:3 (1000x1500). Scene: a cozy unmade bed with rumpled cream linen sheets, soft morning light filtering through a sheer curtain, a phone face-down on a nightstand showing a glowing screen, a half-drunk glass of water nearby, very calm and warm.

memeText:
Got dressed. Made coffee. Forgot both.
This is fine.
title: Funny ADHD memes about morning routines we know too well
description: Starting the day with ADHD means doing five things and finishing none of them - including drinking your coffee while it's hot. Working memory, you had one job. More at bloomfocus.org
imagePrompt: Realistic aesthetic photograph, soft pastel tones (lavender, cream, blush, sage), cozy minimal scene, lots of empty space, soft natural light, shallow depth of field. No people, no text. Vertical 2:3 (1000x1500). Scene: a forgotten mug of coffee on a light wood kitchen counter, soft steam barely visible, a cozy sage green sweater draped over a stool nearby, morning window light casting a gentle glow, very still and quiet atmosphere.
